title: Top 100 Kastner Economic Calls: Ranked by Per-Study Share-of-Prescience Net Attributed Value
author: Peter S. Kastner (subject/reviewer) and Perplexity Computer (methodology architect)
abstract: This 2026 ranking exercise extends the kastner-prescience-methodology-demo by allocating Phase 4 theme-level net-attributed value (share-of-prescience discounted, 2026 USD) across the 360 individual high-prescience studies classified to the 15 headline themes, producing a ranked top-100 list of individual Kastner economic calls. Equal-within-theme allocation is used as the defensible default; ties are by design and indicate the floor of what can be inferred without per-study lead/contrarian/specificity scoring. The exercise demonstrates that the archive supports study-level economic attribution analysis and surfaces specific high-impact items (Mobile/Smartphone calls at $237.7B/study, Digital Consumer Technology at $108.6B/study, OLTP at $57.8B/study, Cloud Computing at $47.4B/study) for future per-study refinement passes.
importance_rationale: First study-level economic ranking in the archive. Extends the methodology demo by drilling from theme-level to study-level allocation. Establishes a defensible floor for per-study valuation and identifies refinement candidates.
relevance_rationale: Directly reusable: any future researcher applying the methodology to a new theme can produce a comparable per-study ranking with one CSV transformation. Surfaces specific top-rank studies for deeper case-study analysis.
prescience_rationale: This is a retrospective ranking exercise, not a forecast.
